seniority.level: Senior Manager / Director-adjacent IC or people manager
level_signals: Experience building teams: recruiting, mentoring, and developing team members
Executive communication and managing up to most senior leaders
Comp range $170K-$250K base consistent with Senior Manager level at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 8+ years of relevant management experience, ideally within a Marketplace or Subscription-focused business and/or founder experience
You're a builder. You have experience scaling products and initiatives from 0 to 1 and are eager to have end to end ownership
You have a track record of impact . You've been in the trenches and have a proven track record of driving significant, company-wide impact.
You have a bias to action. You move quickly and thrive in a fast-paced environment. No job is beneath you and you lead from the front.
You are skilled in executive management and communication, especially at managing up and across DoorDash and going toe to toe with our most senior leaders.
You're unafraid of ambiguity. Rapid change and learning is exciting to you, and you thrive in ambiguous, startup environments.
You have demonstrated experience in building the team necessary to fulfill the strategy. Proactively involved in recruiting, mentoring, and developing team members.
Partner closely with cross-functional teams on product, engineering, data science and marketing
Experiment. You'll transform strategies into operational experiments that create measurable and actionable outcomes, informing your larger initiative implementation plans.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 8+ years of relevant management experience, ideally within a Marketplace or Subscription-focused business and/or founder experience
You have 8+ years of relevant management experience, ideally within a Marketplace or Subscription-focused business and/or founder experience

scope.surface: DashPass — DoorDash's global subscription program; focus on new benefit incubation, user engagement and retention
scope.business_model: B2C subscription (DashPass) within marketplace / on-demand delivery platform
core_responsibilities: Lead strategy and execution for DashPass marketplace business
Own new benefit incubation to drive user engagement and retention
Drive 0 to 1 initiatives: experimentation, launch, and scaling of net new benefits
Transform strategies into operational experiments with measurable outcomes
Partner with cross-functional teams on product, engineering, data science, and marketing
Communicate strategy and results to all audiences including senior leadership

green_flags: High-visibility role owning a flagship product (DashPass) with clear scope
End-to-end ownership of 0-to-1 initiatives described clearly
Equity grants included in compensation package
Comprehensive benefits package disclosed (parental leave, 401k match, wellness)
red_flags: Location/work arrangement is not stated in the JD body — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
The JD references NYC-specific AEDT (Covey) tool disclosures but does not clarify if the role is NYC-based or remote-NYC-associated, creating location ambiguity
Many requirements are written as cultural/personality traits ('breakthrough walls mentality', 'bias to action') rather than concrete, verifiable skills — assessment criteria may be subjective

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement, comp range, and people management signals)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'building the team' and 'recruiting, mentoring, developing team members')
scope.business_model (inferred from DashPass subscription product description)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
ambiguities: Location is entirely unstated in the role body. The JD includes NYC AEDT (Covey) notices for 'NYC or Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC Only', which could imply the role is NYC-based or NYC-remote-associated, but this is not confirmed.

The years requirement ('8+ years of relevant management experience') is in a section labeled 'We're excited about you because…' alongside softer traits — treated as hard given the unconditional phrasing and specificity of the year threshold.
Marketplace/Subscription background and founder experience are framed as 'ideally' which signals nice-to-have, but the broader 8+ years clause is hard — extracted as two separate items accordingly.
